original: You summon [flurry.name] to the central chamber of [frostwyrm.name]’s lair, where you and [frostwyrm.name] reside. Only a few seconds later, the sound of his heavy footsteps starts echoing off the smooth, icy walls, and you can feel the tremors of his footfalls as he makes his way towards the two of you. Another moment later and you see his body reflecting off the glassy ice before he rounds the final corner. His head is tilted to one side as he approaches. How can I assist, my Qal? he asks. [frostwyrm.name]’s head rises from her sitting position beside you, eyeing [flurry.name] as he approaches. When he nears the foot of the dais you two are on, he sits on his haunches, awaiting your further instruction; she, likewise, twists her long neck to face you patiently. You had summoned him to the central chamber; whatever it is you wanted clearly involves them both. There’s no easy way to lead into it: you tell them both that there’s a serious matter you need to address, and insisting that it’s not a problem is only going to make it worse. The world is running out of pureblood Frostwyrms. As soon as the words leave your mouth, you feel a deep, cold pit form in your chest. It’s not your sensation: it’s [frostwyrm.name]’s and [flurry.name]’s. It’s a reality that they had both been trying to ignore since you first met them – when the topic came up, they would instead try to focus on the better things they had in life, such as finally have a mate of their own, but it’s time you three stopped kicking the can down the road, as it were. This is no easy topic to discuss, [frostwyrm.name] warns. Her tone is unusually low and stern, as if she were trying to intimidate you into stopping – but her words come to you shakily. Her conviction is shot: she knows that you’re right, but she’s still trying to fight it. You are our Qal, [pc.name], yet your origin is with a kind that has never known the struggles that [flurry.name] and I face. You cannot understand the difficulties it is that we endure. That’s exactly why you three need to have this conversation. [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] have one particular thing in common: they only took you as their mate because they were desperate. In another life, if they had waited just a little longer, they would have found each other, and you three wouldn’t be in this predicament. I do not regret my choice, [frostwyrm.name] insists. Nor I, [flurry.name] continues. That you are not of our kind, our Qal, is no longer a concern to either of us. Yes, you know that, but that’s not the point you’re trying to make. They are both viable, pureblood Frostwyrms. No matter how many kips you give to either of them, you’re simply not a Frostwyrm yourself[pc.ra frostwyrm | – not pureblood, anyway ]. It’s beautiful and rewarding and wholesome for them to say that they’re so enamored with you and that you’ve been a good Qal to them, but... given that the Frostwyrms are dying out, they have to make a decision that they may not be comfortable with. One that stretches how they feel about wanting a ‘typical clutch’ even further. You are suggesting that [flurry.name] and I mate, [frostwyrm.name] says flatly, cutting through all the fluff you’ve been setting up between them. ... Yes, that’s what you’ve been working towards. [frostwyrm.name]’s eyes close, and she takes a long, deep sigh through her nose. Given her size, it lasts nearly a full minute. What you are asking is not impossible, she eventually relents. Her eyes remain closed. Though... it is not so simple a matter as one of us mounting the other. Isn’t it? Our situation is atypical, our Qal, [flurry.name] answers for her. [frostwyrm.andNykke |[frostwyrm.name], Nykke, and I |[frostwyrm.name] and I ] entered into a clutch with more than one Qim out of necessity. A Qal and a Qim mating with each other is not merely a matter of copulation: it is about choosing your partner and remaining with them until you are summoned by the Grand Qim. Our clutch is already atypical and includes multiple Qims, but the idea that a Qim would mate with another Qim is... implausible. You don’t want to seem like you’re insensitive or ignorant, but you don’t understand the issue. You won’t deny that there’s an emotional aspect to it, but the way [flurry.name] describes it, it’s almost like he’s saying it physically can’t be done. It may not be, he replies. It is you that has taken the role of Qal, [pc.name]. [frostwyrm.name] and I – our kind[frostwyrm.andNykke |, and Nykke as well ] – we do not mate for recreation. Like your kind, we require tactile feedback to ejaculate, but we cannot experience that feedback without a mate. And if we cannot ejaculate, we cannot procreate. ... And since he and [frostwyrm.name] aren’t mates, he’s suggesting that they wouldn’t feel pleasure if they mated with each other? Were a Qim, for example, to be summoned by the Grand Qim before their Qal, the widowed Qal could not take a new Qim for this same reason. It goes against our... understanding of what a clutch is meant to be. More for your own curiosity than you trying to think of ways [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] could have sex with each other, you ask if they couldn’t they just... pretend? Couldn’t [frostwyrm.name] take on the role of the Qal for a little bit or something? That is impossible, [flurry.name] responds. It is you that is the Qal. [frostwyrm.name]’s been quiet for a little while since you had started, and when you look to her, you see that her eyes have been cast down towards the icy floor. Her breathing is shallow to the point of being nearly indistinct: it’s like she’s trying to hide from you. You ask her if she’s okay. She takes a long moment to herself before answering. You have been asking questions about the plausibility of the act, my Qal, she answers slowly. You feel a cold pit in your chest as she speaks, but you didn’t need to feel that to know that she’s upset. I will not chastise you for your misunderstandings and your efforts to right them, but there is an emotive aspect to this that we are each neglecting, even if it were possible for me to assume the role of Qal temporarily. She rotates her head and lifts her snout so that her two ruby-red eyes look into yours. You are my Qal. [flurry.name] is not my Qal. And I cannot, and would not, assume the role of Qal. The air hangs heavy, her words lingering between you and [flurry.name]. You are the Qal to [frostwyrm.andNykke |three |two ] dragons, and all that entails: as Qal, you are the teacher to your offspring, meant to pass on the knowledge and traditions of earlier generations to the next – but you are also the mate to your Qim. Having to share you isn’t easy for her, just as it hasn’t been easy for [flurry.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | and Nykke ]. Our Qal speaks the truth, [flurry.name] says, before his voice erupts in a series of impossible vowels and consonants that you recognize as [frostwyrm.name]’s true name. Our kind is in peril. We cannot continue to ignore this. I know, she answers. To not do what our Qal suggests would consign our kind to its fate. I know! she barks, her head lunging upward and her jaw snapping shut in [flurry.name]’s direction. Our Qal and I had this very discussion before we chose to seek you out, she says before her own voice spouts a bunch of gibberish that you recognize as [flurry.name]’s true name. Every time I look at you, I am reminded of the reality that we face: that you and I may well be the last of our kind in this world! At that, you feel a second, stronger, colder pit in your chest – this time, coming from [flurry.name]. It’s not that her words have cut into him: it’s that he feels the same way. No matter how much they adore you as their Qal, the fact remains that, through circumstances neither of them could control, their species is dying and they had both missed an opportunity to not only try and fix that, but to live normal lives and have a normal Frostwyrm family – something they both coveted before they met you. Your hand goes to [frostwyrm.name]’s chest; as soon as she feels your palm rest against her scales, she calms down and lowers her head back to the ice. If she doesn’t want to do this, then she doesn’t want to do this. That’s the end of the discussion. As with all things, it is not that simple, she says, letting out a long, deep sigh – her vocal chords vibrate as she does, and it comes out as a low-pitched whine. There is nothing I desire more than to conserve my kind, our Qal. I am willing to do whatever it takes. I am willing to do anything, but this simply is not a matter of willpower. Her eyes go to [flurry.name], then to yours. It is not right, she continues, her voice lilting in your head. It is not right that we are put into this scenario. It is a cost that the Grand Qim has deigned we remunerate for the gifts we have been given. Again, the air hangs silent for a long moment. [flurry.name], growing emotionally weary from the discussion, stands; takes a step backward; and then lies on his stomach, his snout only about a foot away from [frostwyrm.name]’s. This is a difficult discussion for them both and you don’t want to make light of the scenario by suggesting that sex is the answer... but they have two options: they can accept that there’s nothing that can be done, and [flurry.name] returns to his corner of the lair, and you all go back to the way things were before you had this discussion... ... or, if they’re serious about wanting to conserve their species before it risks going extinct, they mate with each other – and you get involved. That ought to at least solve the issue about them not being able to experience the ‘tactile feedback’ they need to actually get the gears turning, so to speak. [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t immediately respond to your suggestion. When she does, she turns her eyes to you without lifting her head. What are you suggesting? she asks. Well, you’re the Qal, right? They’ll only get the feedback they need if you’re involved. What you three could do is, [pc.hasCock |[frostwyrm.name] mounts [flurry.name], and you mount [frostwyrm.name] on top of them both[pc.hasVagina |. Or, if they’d rather, ] ][pc.hasVagina |[flurry.name] mounts [frostwyrm.name], and [frostwyrm.name] mounts you on the bottom ]; that way, since you’re involved, they’d both get the feedback they’d need, and [frostwyrm.name] still gets to mate with her Qal. And, on top of that, [pc.hasCock |[frostwyrm.name] would get another kip of her own with you as well[pc.hasVagina |, or, if you’re on bottom, ] ][pc.hasVagina |you’d get another kip with [frostwyrm.name][pc.isPregnant |, at least once you pass the kid you’re incubating right now, ] ] to add to your growing clutch. It’s not a perfect solution... but if you three want to do anything about Frostwyrms being endangered, they’ll all just have to accept that no ‘perfect solution’ exists. Both [frostwyrm.name] and [flurry.name] remain silent as they consider your words. Both of them look to you, and, after a moment, their eyes turn to each other. [flurry.name] raises his head off the ice slightly, slowly becoming animated the longer he considers the idea. This would solve the feedback concern, he admits. [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t respond, but from the way she’s looking up at [flurry.name], it’s clear that she isn’t terribly opposed to the idea. Like you said, it’s not a perfect solution, but your involvement would, at the very least, make things a bit easier. I am willing to try, she says eventually. For the sake of the conservation of our kind... I am willing to try. [flurry.name] doesn’t object either. It’s worth a shot.
Next
Next

Before you get started, though, you ask [flurry.name] to return to his section of the lair for a moment. You have something you’d like to discuss with [frostwyrm.name], and you’d prefer it if he was out of earshot for it – and you ask [frostwyrm.name] to block your psionic link with him for privacy. As you say, our Qal, he says, pulling himself to his six legs and heading off towards the rear of the lair. He’s out of eyesight soon enough, but you wait until you stop feeling the vibrations of his feet and you stop hearing the thundering echoes of his footsteps before you start. Once you’re confident that you and [frostwyrm.name] are alone, you reposition yourself so that you’re closer to her neck. She curls her long head towards you, her four eyes trained tightly on you, giving you her undivided attention. You ask her plainly: was it wrong to bring him into the clutch as another Qim[frostwyrm.andNykke |? Was it wrong to bring in Nykke ]? Would she have preferred that she was the only Qim in the clutch? The question seems to have caught her off-guard: she rears her head back, her eyes widening, and she doesn’t immediately respond. Her eyes remain solely on yours, laser-locked and unshakeable as she seriously considers her answer. What feels like a minute passes before she exhales, and her focus on you is broken with a long, slow blink. Yes, she eventually answers. Just as you are my only Qal, I would have preferred that I was your only Qim. But, she continues before you can respond, it was not wrong to bring in [flurry.name][frostwyrm.andNykke | and Nykke ]. These two thoughts are not exclusive to each other. [frostwyrm.andNykke |They were |He was ] hurting as I had been hurting: our scenario is unusual and desperate, and we must all make desperate decisions to adapt. If you were to ask [flurry.name] the same question, there is no doubt that he would respond likewise. She leans her gigantic head towards you; the side of her snout gently rubs against your body, nuzzling you affectionately. It is kind of you to consider my emotions as you have, she continues. I am, as of yet, uncertain of how our attempts to mate me with [flurry.name] will transpire, but no matter what, it is comforting to know that I will have my Qal before, during, and after the attempt. You’re certainly not going anywhere. Qims and Qals mate for life, after all.
Call Nykke

original: Birthing!
Just as your thoughts were turning to your rotund and overbearing belly, you feel something in your loins, followed by a rush in your lower stomach. It’s time; the kip’s on its way right now.

“It was nice being with you, Grimald. See you soon.” [rand |Before he can reply, his radio flares. “Grimald, we need your help near the stadium. We have a ten forty-three.” The feathery boy sighs. “No respite for the brave.” What’s a ten forty-three?” you ask. “[silly |A milodan doing the helicopter. Yes, it’s frequent enough to have its own code |Milodan and korgonne infighting, nothing dire else it’d be an eight twelve. ]” Not wasting a second, he fetches a small medical bag and places a thic[silly |c |k ], anat-like metallic helmet over his face, making him look even more like a fuzzy penguin. He [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |kisses you softly |waves at you ] before soaring through the sky towards the south. You’ll need to catch this free bird one day. |He barely has the time to [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |give you a kiss |blow you a kiss ] before a group of armored warriors bring him a whining huskar. “What happened to him?” you ask. Their gruff leader turns to face you. “That’s what I’d like to know, [pc.mf |sir |M’Lady ].” Grimald sights and reaches for a green pill in his pockets. “You drank a Frisky Dingo, didn’t you?” The dark-furred lupine nods. “We first got some mead, then peanuts, and a Frisky, but he’s the only one in this state.” The avian femboy places the cure in the foaming huskar’s mouth and makes him swallow it. “He should be fine. Some people just can’t handle more than one spirit at a time, especially not after filling their bellies with huskar treats and peanuts. Only give him water for the next three hours and make sure he sleeps for eight hours and he should be fine.” A couple of seconds later, the drooling huskar stops shivering. “I’ll stay with him for now,” Grimald adds. “Y’all should get some rest too.” The brutes shrug and head back to town, certainly to get hammered more. You leave Grimald with his patient, wishing him luck considering how reckless stormguards can be. |He barely has the time to [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |give you a kiss |say goodbye ] before a group of helmeted warriors come to him. “Grimald! We need your help.” “How so?” the cheeky avian smirks. “Let me guess. Duncan said the best hoverbike is the YXT-69 and Hjorr said it was the RS-45-GL while it’s clearly the HK-32?” A horned guardian shakes his head. “No like, can you do a barrel roll then immediately proceed to do a looping without fainting?” “Definitely not without training or without combat stims and even so, I wouldn’t try it if I wasn’t chased by a hypersonic warhead. Don’t even think about doing it with one of our rides,” he almost shouts. “Alright alright. Settle down dude, geez. We won’t touch your stuff this tim-” The huskar of the group shuts him up. “We won’t do it at all. That’s what he meant.” “Suuuuuuure,” Grimald says, eyeing them as they return to the barracks. You’ll have to be careful, the weather’s gonna be snowy with chances of aircraft accidents. ]

		|give him a very angular face
	]. His full lips and round cheeks soften him up a lot, making him look very friendly and cheerful despite the ambient coldness. Soft, human ears sometimes surface from under his hood’s fuzz, blinding you a bit with his golden earrings.

“What brings an exotic bird like you to this cold little moon?” Grimald giggles. “I was born here, silly! I’m a strong lad fresh from the Stormguard’s barracks,” the little guy boasts, like a peacock three times his size. “My family’s been on Uveto since the tenth Planet Rush. Well, my mother’s side of the family to be exact. We can trace our lineage back to the first settlers that made contact with this beautiful place’s friendly and not-so-friendly locals. As for my father, he’s an impressive anat. You might have crossed him in Uveto station. He works with the Camarilla, doing fancy stuff like coordinating ore trade between the empire and the tove.” “So you are a half-breed?” you ask. “Yes, half-anat, half-human. A very fluffy boy if you will,” he teases, waddling in his thic[silly |c |k ], stuffed parka. “My parents met near the radio tower, in the middle of a tribal truce. A truce that turned into a brawl. A drunk milodan chief presumably punched a korgonne envoy who then fell onto my Mom and shit hit the fan. In the blink of an eye, everyone was involved in the melee: Mom’s squad, Dad’s colleagues, local goons; absolutely everyone had a part in this deluge of unrestrained violence. My parents ended up back-to-back, surrounded by enraged korgonnes, with only a depowered thunder lance and a [silly |foldable chair |brutish goad ] left. According to Mom, it was [silly |but a tuesday |a nice challenge ]. As for Dad, it was the closest he was to dying. It was supposed to be a mundane peace agreement between the Camarilla and the western tribes, and it turned crazy.” At least they managed to leave unscathed. Did they get along well afterward? The amber-feathered boy nods. “Mom was impressed by my father’s insane bravery. For her — the fearsome zealot that modded herself to look like a divinely winged maiden — he wasn’t a puny bureaucrat unworthy of her attention anymore. Once the bloody mess was dealt with, she invited him for a quick drink at the Freezer. One thing led to another, they went on a date, then two, then settled together, got married, and had their first child.” You? “No, my big sister, Dahlia. She took a lot more than I did from Father. You wouldn’t differentiate her from a purebreed anat female except for her chestnut eyes. I was more like a happy little accident,” he laughs, this time a bit more dryly. You were not wanted? Grimald sighs. “Let’s just say I wasn’t the perfect boy they expected. They were very demanding. My sister and I had to be the best, no matter how hard it was. I’m lucky Dahlia shielded me from the worst of their misplaced pride and high standards. Fortunately, she excelled way over their expectations. She even got a scholarship for a famous Ausaril college. I, on the other hand, was meeker and shyer. Mom trained me to be a stormguard — which I became eventually, albeit after years in the Terran Space Coalition. But that’s a story for another time,” he says, looking at his augmented arms. Yeah, you can understand what he went through; your Dad and Briget pushed you a lot to become the [pc.race] you are now. Does he have a partner yet? “Interested in me, are you?” he teases before [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |winking at you |kissing you softly ]. “I’m not taken at the moment. Life’s been a bit too busy for that, but I hope to get there one day. I’m sure Mom will melt on the spot knowing I found a mate. She was over the moon when Dahlia presented Sloan, her essyra crush, to her. Can you imagine that? An amazonian valkyrie squealing in front of [silly |an extra thicc |a plus size ] vulpine huntress? Sloan’s cheeks were scarlet from embarrassment, and my sister’s teasing didn’t help. Whenever they are around, Dahlia gropes and praises her spouse in public. Stars, my sister’s a real freak.” She sounds interesting, that’s for sure. His family sounds weird, but the good kind of weird. Since you are on the topic of personal stuff, what does he like? The avian halfbreed smiles. “I really love hot chocolate and candied nuts, as you can see,” he says, raising his fuming cup. “I’m fond of the snow, of how the wind blows in my feathers when the sun sets, of how beautiful the sky looks whenever our distant star grants us a shimmering aurora. I love to pilot vehicles: be it a power-forklift, a vintage hoverbike, or a high-class pleasure yacht, I just love to be in control of a roaring engine. I wasn’t capable of flight for a very long time, so it was the closest I got to experiencing it. Stars, I’d kill to get my hands on our StormRider Skimmer; it’s a large, boat-like hovertank the Stormguard used back when we didn’t have train stations linking the savicite mines and harbors to Irestead. I bet it’s like driving a massive hovertank that’s as deft, fast, and agile as an eagle. At least Sloan lets me ride her kor’diiak when she’s in town. He’s so big and fluffy, a giant teddy bear like his mistress. I’ll try to tame one the next time she invites us to her place.” Is there anything he likes that isn’t related to speed? “I’m also a pretty good belly dancer. Mom didn’t want me to do something so girly, but I managed to learn some skills here and there, between missions. I can give you a private performance, if you want,” he teases. “A private performance? Don’t you feel you’re flying too close to the sun?” “I would never, but if you ever want to bed a birdie, be aware that I’m not that kinky. Sure, I love to pitch and catch, but I’m not one to do tantric acrobatics. I’m rather greedy in terms of nuts though. The more the merrier. I can’t get enough of fresh, alien seed. I’m not well-hung so I’m used to bottoming a lot. I also love getting praised and teased. Stars, I’m so sensitive down there, you won’t believe it without having a sample,” he says before realizing just how much he disclosed about his sex life. He blushes deeply, so flustered he hides his face in his fuzzy clothes. “Sorry sorry, I got a little carried away.” So he likes to be showered in sticky stuff and treated like the good boy he is? You’ll keep that in mind. Grimald slowly sips some hot chocolate, trying to calm himself a little. “I-I’m not just a sub. I can top too! I might not have the meatiest of sausages, but I’ve got balls to make an alpha dzaan blush. Else, I can show you just how cool it feels to play with hot stuff...” Now you see the stormguard in him shining. It wouldn’t be so bad to play with a mythic thunderbird, assuming he can keep up with you. Grimald sighs. “[flags.GRIMALD_SEX |Just don’t ruffle my feathers too much please |Remember to go slow with my feathers ]. They are very sensitive and it takes so much time to clean them[silly | D: |. ]”

“You are working for the Stormguard, correct?” The cute boy nods. “Yep. I’m in the third recon team, the medical intervention and emergency rescue squad. If one of us or somebody that booked our services is in dire need of extraction, we must be ready to scour the tundra, glacier, and even the glacial ocean to find them,” he proudly boasts, making his feathery tail sway behind him. Interesting. “Is it your first job? You look rather young, after all.” “Don’t let my cute face fool you. I’m a big boy: a veteran from the Terran Space Coalition’s fleet.” “Wait, you were in the [silly |Navy |Terran forces ]?” you ask, a bit shocked. “You had the galaxy to explore and you landed here of all places?” Grimald sips some hot chocolate. “Uveto is my homeworld, so it’s not that weird I came back here when I was demobilized. Being in the army was fun. I had lots of free time between ship maintenance, I learned field medic and surgeon skills, and the boys and gals were so chill compared to, well, my parents.” [var0 |You understand. Vic and Briget were also strict and tough with you. But it’s still concerning that his mother and father were stricter than the peacekeeping forces! |The peacekeeping forces had more lenient rules than his parents? He must have had a very strict upbringing! ] “You can’t help it. With a mother in the Stormguard and a father from the Anat Empire, you will have a very demanding schooling. It wasn’t so bad though; in comparison, my time in the army seemed more like a vacation with easy classes from time to time. I even became my ship’s most talented performer. Stars, I loved ceremonial parades and going out on permission with my friends. I spent many days and nights dancing half-naked on top of bar tables, swaying my feathers like a peacock. Everyone called me Chou Chou behind closed doors – and under weighted blankets,” he winks. Well, it sounds like he had a very good time being a soldier. Why did he leave? The avian femboy frowns and raises his right arm. You easily spot the circuitry and hardlight devices laced in his modded flesh. There’s also tiny white scars scattered here and there, almost invisible from afar. “My ship, the Rhadamanthus, escorted a convoy through a recently-secured quadrant. We knew this wouldn’t be a pleasant ride and that we would come across pirates, but since we had three escort ships flanking our spacecraft carrier, we knew only the strongest or most reckless of them would even dare approach us. And they did: halfway through an asteroid field, they rose from the star’s fiery crown and from behind a gaseous planet’s moon at ramming speed, ready to engage us. We immediately recognized their flagship’s emblem: the Black Void.” How did they fare against them? “Badly. By the time we were harnessed and deployed around the convoy, they had already closed their jaws on us. We were trapped between the cold and a hard place, surrounded by three times more ships than we had. Let me just-” Grimald recovers a small holo-device from a hidden pocket and activates it. In the blink of an eye, a projection of a small star system and bundles of triangular tokens lights your surroundings. “Commander Nesm used the Rhadamanthus to shield the smaller transporters from the worst of the missiles, lasers, and thermal cannons blasts,” he says as swarms of small ships surround the biggest vessel. “I lost many friends, but we made them bleed far more,” he mumbles, his face suddenly somber and hurt. “Since we knew conventional maneuvers wouldn’t suffice to push them back, we improvised and used the asteroid fields as hunting grounds,” he says. Meanwhile, the holographic ships sail through the spatial reef. “Between the uneven gravitic disturbances and the deluge of missiles unleashed upon us, it’s a miracle so many pilots survived the raid – even more that so many were mostly unscathed.” But he wasn’t one of them, right? The amber-feathered stormguard nods. “I was a bomber pilot, so I was slower and took way more flak than others,” he replies, pointing at one of the tiny points skittering toward the pirate battleships. “I barely had the time to fire electromagnetic torpedoes at our enemies’ bulkiest cruiser before a lumpy corvette hit me with its coil cannons. My shields broke and I felt something sting my arms, but I didn’t see anything. Since we couldn’t afford to lose what little armor-piercing capabilities the fleet had, I continued to fight with a growing ache in my hands, firing my [silly |lazor |ordnance ] in the hope of crippling them with a direct hit to a reactor or thruster. Luckily for us, a patrolling fleet arrived in the system at the right moment.” “The Black Void might be organized and have a rigid, almost military structure, but they remain outlaws, and outlaws are innately selfish. Their troops’ coherency crumbled, which allowed us to easily cripple their biggest ships. It was a bitter victory, but this could have been far worse. Fighting the debilitating pain, I landed on the Rhadamanthus and was immediately taken by the crew’s medics. I didn’t understand at the time because of the adrenaline, but I was on the verge of losing my arms.” Losing his arms? Was it because of the corvette’s blow? “I did lose them in some way. My nerves and ligaments were almost severed by minuscule, subatomic debris, and the long space fight didn’t help. Thousands of tiny needles pierced my flesh and bones. What makes it a particularly vicious weapon is that they slowly dissolve with time, taking skin, bones, ligaments, and muscles with them. The medical crew operated on me in an emergency, between a badly-burned kaithrit and an officer crushed by a command console. In the end, the surgeons replaced about eighty percent of my hands’ flesh and bones with cybernetics. My left thumb is the only finger attached to a fleshy muscle for instance, while most of my right side is fine flesh-wise but my bones were all replaced. They did a great job fixing me, considering the situation at the time and what little means they had. I used a fair share of my savings to add hardlight feathers projectors to them – I didn’t want people to look at my arms and be sad, especially since I was the cheerful one; the one entertaining others and putting a smile on people’s faces.” So after this accident, he was demobilized and returned to Uveto? “My contract came to an end two weeks after I left the medical bay, and I didn’t plan to stay much longer anyway. I made a promise to one of my comrades: to stay the same, happy ray of sunshine no matter what and to always follow my heart. So I took my disability pension and returned home for the first time in three years. Mom hugged me for five minutes straight when I showed up with my snow-covered uniform. At the time, they were looking for a new pilot to drive the recovery shuttle, so I applied for the position and passed with flying colors,” he proudly boasts. What does his job entail? Does he have to do only ship-related tasks or does he need to fight as well? The harpy boy shrugs. “A bit of both. I need to be ready to fight anything that threatens my fellow stormguards’ integrity. Since I’m the medical team’s pilot, I need to know how to drive carefully and quickly enough to bring people back to safety and heal them before they either die from their wounds or freeze to death. It’s not unusual for my colleagues to operate on people in the middle of a flight. It’s rather bone-chilling to know that you might have either been too slow to save them from the cold or too fast to allow them to perform emergency procedures.” Does it happen often? “Not really, no. Most of the time, we are called to rescue somebody from the blizzard or to transport tourists back to Irestead’s dispensary after a bad fall. It’s rare that we need to do surgery on the spot. Discovering the true state of our patient is always a surprise — and usually not a pleasant one. You know it’ll be a long day when you reach for the heavy duty tools before the litter.” Did he have to operate somebody on the glacier? Grimald nods. “Once. The alpha team was greatly injured and needed to be evacuated immediately. It was dark and the blizzard was dense. They didn’t realize they were into Kor’diiak hunting grounds before the mighty beasts surrounded them. No matter how strong and tough you are, you are no match against an ursus gigantus — let alone eight of them.” “Two stormguards, Ymgir and Fjoor, were in pretty bad shape. The alien bears focused on them because they were the shortest and bulkiest of the squad. The former, a dwarfish huskar, needed oxygen after one of the apex predators collapsed on him while his gigantic companion took the worst of their punishment defending him. To this day, I don’t know how we managed to lift the kor’diiak off Ymgir, let alone stabilize Fjorr. I only remember red blotches and my racing breaths turning into wet clouds. It took us an hour of desperate work to be able to move him back to the ship. Nobody in the medical team slept until they both awakened in the dispensary. It’s a miracle they got away with just a few scars and broken bones.” All’s well that ends well. After all, they were in very good hands. “S-Sure,” Grimald replies, blushing slightly. “I’ve still room to improve but even I was surprised I could perform so well under pressure. My arms might be mostly artificial, but the work they can do is genuine.” What does he do when he isn’t dispatched? “I usually spend time polishing the shuttle’s sides, rigging the antigrav engine, checking the onboard medic kits, and waiting for an emergency call. We’ve got one or two per week on average, but we aren’t safe from a brutal avalanche or a mining shaft accident. There’s also the more mundane incidents like exhibitionism or bar brawls, but I usually take care of the aftermath.” “Exhibitionism? In this climate?” Grimald giggles. “It’s more likely than you’d think. Between the sauna users thinking they’re invincible or drunkards that got one too many glasses at the Freezer, we’ve got a lot of work to do. We usually arrive before the sheriff, not that they can do much against the cold. A quick sip of firewater, a warm coat, and a heat belt usually suffice to fix the problem, but it’s good to have the sobering cells at hand. Our hold is more of a fancy monastery than a proper police station or hospital. We do, however, have nice kitchens and delicious roasted meats,” he says, clearly not thinking about food. Did he get in one of his friends’ pants? “Not yet,” he replies, hiding his blushing cheeks behind his mug. “Relations between members of the same teams aren’t forbidden, but we are enticed to spread and reproduce if you know what I mean. Stars, there’s so many hung dudes and chesty gals in here. You should see our sexy calendars... and the showers! Oh, the communal showers!” You cough, stopping the little bird’s daydreaming. The small bulge in his trousers twitches through his layered fabric. “I see your ship isn’t the only thing you polish.” “I’d love to have some fun sometimes. No matter how fine the snow is and how good the chocolate tastes, the nights are still long and cold, albeit beautiful. One day, I’ll find my special someone. Somebody who’ll hold me tight against them, give me a kiss before going to work, and help me groom my feathers. For someone like that, I’d do anything, even give them my arms.” “How would you even fly without them?” you retort. Grimald smirks. “I wouldn’t be against being a househusband, but my team will surely miss me. Some knew me since the cradle. I’m almost a nephew to the main medical officer. He told me multiple times he’d like me to finish my studies to take his place when the time comes. I’m not ready yet to return to school — and don’t have the funds for it — so that settles the issue for now, but I wouldn’t be against having a better degree than my sister. Until then, I’ll remain a fine pilot and field medic.”

“What do you like about Uveto? There must be something special about this place that keeps you here.” The avian femboy sips some hot chocolate before answering. “There’s lots of things that make me feel at home here. First, there’s my family and childhood friends. You know how the saying goes: There’s no place like home. I’ve been to many places during my time in the terran fleet: Ausaril’s dry plains, Maggrath’s tropical jungles, Earth’s lush climate, Anon-Xusya B’s electromagnetic tempests, Dragarea’s volcanic hellscape, but nothing compares to my homeworld’s chill embrace. I liked Caphist — dad’s homeworld — too, but it’s not really the same. As much as I love the anat, their society makes my skin crawl.” How so? “Well... that they’re raised together, some not even knowing who their parents were. I know it helps them become their own person, but for someone who came from a nuclear family, I can’t understand how they build their identity without knowing their parents. A childhood friend of mine was adopted by an uplifted milodan couple. He was loved as much as his other siblings but he still needed to look for his biological parents, so I can only imagine how it must be for bevy-grown birdies.” You were raised mostly by Briget, but your parents were here when you needed them. You can understand him somewhat. Is there something he loves specifically about his family? Grimald thinks for a couple of seconds. “Mom used to bring us with her all around the moon. Dahlia, my sister, and I did lots of sledding and ice fishing with her. I remember the day we caught a forty pound anormalocaris. We unfortunately had to release the huge crustacean, but we brought a big scarlet cod home. Uveto has lots of arctic seafood, but most of the aquatic species are protected for scientific purposes. We don’t actually know which ones are at risk of extinction, because we only know a handful of them. The ecosystem is so rich, despite the rabid mining exploitation and the many problems it creates.” What was it like to grow up on Uveto? “It was fine. There were lots of snowball fights and snowman-building contests. I’m not very good at ice skating, but my sister was very skilled. She could have been a galactic champion if she wanted but she was more of a hockey player, with her broad shoulders and powerful legs. The Stormguard uses a good chunk of its credits to fund the sports team and school activities, to justify its social usefulness and buy themselves some respect from the less religious-inclined folks. There’s a yearly festival in Irestead with cheap local food, mead, games, and traditional music; tribal korgonne, milodans, and essyra usually participate as well and trade their goods with us.” Sounds fun. “That’s how my sister met her wife: they bumped together on the ice ring and she bought her a flask of thunderous firewater and cream-filled buns to apologize. I remember how Sloan’s cheeks turned crimson when Dahlia told her she’d like to taste essyra buns later.” “I see. Maybe I’ll need a date to guide me through the festival,” you tease, making his tail feathers sway happily behind him. “S-Sure. I also love how peaceful this place can be. The ice floe and tundra might be often scoured by blizzards and thunderstorms, but most of the time, only a dim breeze fills the empty plains and sharp canyons. When the sky’s clear, the starlight reflects on the layered ice and the frozen ores, turning the soil and mountains into an ethereal mix of colors. Legends say that when the fog settles and the glacier turns green, vengeful ghosts rise from the oceans below to drag travelers to their doom.” Are these stories true? Grimald shrugs. “No idea. I’ve been in one already but I saw no spirit or banshee. I did, however, rescue a stormguard warrior that wailed like one. Turns out she merely scratched her wrists, but her savicite piercings made her so sensitive that the slightest sting sufficed to trigger a masochistic trip in her pent-up mind. Pro tip: if you use savicite to enhance your psionic powers or to increase your bedroom fun, don’t forget to vent some steam; it’ll save you a very embarrassing visit to the doc. You wouldn’t believe what people use to relieve themselves when the green fever gets to them.” Did he, well... “No no no no. Fucking a delirious patient is out of question. We’ve got chill pills for preventive treatment and lucinite restraints for the most heat-addled minds. Nothing like a cool touch to quench the wildest of fires. Speaking of fiery events, we are approaching the aurora season. They are wonderful, especially when they reflect on the icy waters. The world looks like a kaleidoscopic swirl of colors. It’s absolutely mind-blowing. There’s even a spot where the electromagnetic waves interact with ancient pylons to create lightning jolts. It attracts lots of tourists.” [flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |“I saw them already with some friends. The sight was so magical from above.” “Above? You crossed the glacial canyons to get there? Only the bravest korgonne follow that path. You’re bolder or more reckless than I thought.” You smirk. “I like to live dangerously. And besides, I was well-escorted,” you tease, leaning closer to him. “Are there other places worthy of interest?” |“Are there other places of interest?” ] The male harpy thinks a little. “There’s the coral reefs and wonders of the depths if you aren’t scared of the dark – or from drowning. Some say an autonomous janeria colony claimed a lost depthsuit and now roams the ocean floor in search of targets to leech or, in the worst cases, assimilate. They’re only rumors, but it wouldn’t surprise me if they were true. For what it’s worth, there might be an entire underwater civilization under our feet. We barely scratched the surface of what’s under the planet’s icy crust, maybe there’s a hollow cavity between two layers of Uveto’s core.” It sounds unlikely. He talked about sledding earlier, does he have a favorite spot? “North-west of here, there’s a big, natural slope. Everyone from kids to old ones go there to ski, surf, and glide down the snowy canyon. There’s three tracks available: an easy one for beginners, an intermediate for seasoned riders, and a pro side for the bravest souls. It’s been a while since I went there, but I’d gladly take a free day to go there with [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |you, [pc.name] |a special someone ].” That sounds neat. You’ll think about it.

She’s very important to you, for personal reasons. “I see. I knew this snowkitty had a secret life,” he teases. “She’s a lucky woman. At least most of them are in good shape and remember their self-defense training when things get serious, else I’d be constantly on the move between the savicite mines, shores, and research facilities.” Fortunately, that’s not the case. Did he work with Shade already? The avian stormguard shakes his head again. “She does most of her business off-world, but I wouldn’t mind being her pilot. Stars, I’d love to test her MS-XI. You can climb the Stormguard hierarchy by ritual combat and some members take it very seriously, very very seriously. This charming valkyrie over here is Maggna Woolfson, the Unbreakable Flame, and incidentally, my mother.” W-What? She’s his mother... so the one that stopped you... The tall man pulls his hood back, revealing his sharp anat face and bright red feathers. “Na’dyr Kuhatl, my father.” That’s a lot to unpack. A moment ago, he was fighting for his life and now he somewhat presents you to his parents? What a [dayNight |day |night ]! Maggna pushes a stray braid aside. “Wait... [pc.race], [pc.mf |handsome |pretty ], that looks like [pc.heShe]’s from another world... Grimald, is this your new [pc.mf |boy |girl ]friend?” “[pc.name] and I... we-” You shut him up with a kiss. “Here. It should suffice.” The blonde valkyrie all but melts on the spot. “Gods, they are so cute together. Maybe it’s the right one this time. Maybe I’m gonna be a gr- Wait... before I forget, I have one question for you, [pc.name] – and don’t you dare lie to me.” Na’dyr walks by her side. “Easy there Maggie, don’t threaten Grimald’s friend. Please [pc.name], be honest, even if it hurts.” Oh dear, what is she gonna ask you? Maggna looks you straight in the eyes. “Are you the top or the bottom in the relationship?”

Top

[top |Na’dir |Maggna ] places the credit chip on the countertop and orders four pints. The greyhound slowly pours a black, liquorous liquid into oak tankards. Once they are filled to the brim, he squeezes four droplets of a dark red root into each glass and hands them to you. Grimald blinks. “Taking the soft option? It must be a very important day.” “Your friend saw me angry today and I don’t intend [pc.himHer] to see me drunk,” his mother replies. “Cheers, kids.” Here goes nothing. You give the drink a taste and are immediately assaulted by its mellowed sweetness. It distantly reminds you of liquorice with just a hint of spiciness, or fermented mint brewed with honey and maple syrup. “It’s like drinking cotton candy.” “That’s Stormguard root beer for you,” Na’dyr squawks. “It’s more of a trout to be honest. Strong and hot, so sweet and spicy.” Maggna smirks. “Are you still talking about the drink?” The [silly |bird dilf |robed anat ] raises an eyebrow before setting his eyes on you. They are the same blue as Grimald’s but his bone mask makes them look a lot more intense and menacing. “So tell me [pc.name] — what do you do for a living?” [pc.isBimbo |Oh gosh, a question you know the answer to! ][pc.isBro |Easy question, easy answer. ]”I’m a Steele Tech scout. I explore the new worlds in search of minerals and assets to claim,” you explain. “It’s a high risk, high reward job.” “Oh! I like that! Wait, what are you doing here then? Shouldn’t you be in some alien wonderworld right now?” the blonde valkyrie inquires. “I sometimes bring back genetic samples to our Irestead outpost. They have a great bioengineering lab,” you reply. Na’dyr nods. “Indeed. I think their lead developer tries all their products on himself. I’ve never seen him with the same body for more than a few weeks. He’s a very good customer. Half of the caphistian grenades I import end up in his office.” “I wonder where the others go,” Grimald impishly teases before taking another gulp. “Stars, I could drink Yggdrasil all day.” “Be careful or it’ll all go down to your rear,” you murmur, squeezing his plush butt. “Your root beer is very sweet, almost liquorous. It’s hard to believe it comes from Uveto.” Grimald’s mother proudly pumps her chest. “The original recipe was conceived by the first stormguard-korgonne couple on a long winter night. The formula and their love crossed the ages, encouraging us to do our best and live our lives to the fullest.” You wrap an arm around Grimald’s waist. “Maybe we should conceive our own wonder too. Something so beautiful it’ll be remembered until the stars turn dark and cold.” “[pc.name]. I don-” You shut Grimald with a kiss. The cyborg nurse smooches you back, making his mom squeal. “—! You’re playing a very dangerous game.” “It makes it all the more exciting,” you reply before taking a deep sip. “I’m growing fond of this stuff.” “Maybe we should give you some intimacy,” Maggna says, hooking her arm around her husband’s waist. “We do have our own date to finish after all.” Na’dyr finishes his drink and gives his wife a kiss. “It was nice meeting you [pc.name]. May the winds carry you to the stars and beyond.” Before you can say anything, Maggna sweepes the robed anat off his feet and deploys her wings. “Don’t mind if I do. Farewell kids, don’t stay out in the cold too long!” With that, the blonde valkyrie soars through the sky on pristine wings. That was... something. Grimald really has a weird family, a weird but endearing one. For a first time with them, things went pretty well. Your date slowly breathes out, as if someone took a huge burden off his shoulders. “I... [pc.name]. Y-” You place a finger on his soft lips. “Shhhsh, it’s fine. I met your parents and we are still alive — on the verge of sugar rush but alive and well. It was a pleasant surprise.” The half-anat nods. “Mom can be a handful. I’m glad that you didn’t run away when she wasn’t looking. You had her wrapped around your finger in a heartbeat and my father was under your spell too. What’s your secret?” “I’m just that good,” you wryly answer. “Also, there’s nothing on Uveto that can move me away from this hand-magnet,” you tease, cupping Grimald’s plump rear. The frisky bird reaches for your [pc.lips]. He holds you tight against his chest, hands on your [pc.isTaur |sides |hips ], tongues enlaced, dancing a suave tango. You moan softly, firmly squishing his callipygian rump while you share the messiest kiss in the galaxy. You don’t breathe, you don’t think, you just love, and that’s all that matters. He’s your hot boytoy and you are his drooling [pc.mf |stud |chick ], slaves to desire, enamored to one another, lost in bliss. After a long minute of intense frenching, you finally release the cute anat. You are panting, breathless, charmed, intoxicated, in love. One word for him and you’ll stay here with him, forever. Reluctantly, Grimald ends the kiss and takes your hands in his chromed palms. “I’ll remember this [dayNight |day |night ] the rest of my life. Thank you so much [pc.name].” “You’re welcome, hot wings. I think we need some rest after this, time to digest all the delicious stuff we ate and drank. We’ll certainly need to do lots of exercise to burn all these calories away.” He smirks. “I’ll happily help you with that. You can call me anytime if you need a little workout.” You give him another kiss. “I’ll hold to that word. See you later, handsome.” Grimald blows you a kiss. “Come back soon. I’ll keep some hot cocoa for you.” Hot cocoa and a sweet caramel boy? He’s really spoiling you. If he continues on like this, you might keep him all to yourself.

You can hear the motors and pistons in his hands roar with power as he arms himself. Oh stars, he isn’t playing this time! You duck at the last second, slipping down just before the snowball hits! It misses you by a hair’s breadth before ending its race right in Grimald’s face, knocking him head over ass! The orange bird shakes his head, a little stunned by the aftershock. You quickly turn around only to realize that two new challengers joined the fight — two challengers that you know [flags.DAHLIA_SEX_ACTS_DONE |VERY well |[flags.SLOAN_SEX_ACTS_DONE |VERY well |well ] ]. “D-Dahlia, not in the face!” Sloan murmurs. The anat woman smiles wryly. “Don’t worry Sloan, my lil’ bro isn’t made out of sugar. This bad boy could fly a burning spaceship with a concussion and internal bleeding, isn’t that right Grimmie?” Your date slowly stands, dusting himself and shaking the snow out of his hair. “Correct. Nice seeing you too, big sis.” The feathered chick makes a playful nod before setting her eyes on you. “Hello [pc.name]. I hope you’ve got a better aim than him because I have a snowball with your name on it.” Oh really? Since you are four, maybe you could make it a team fight? Grimald and you versus Sloan and her? “Are you sure about that [pc.name]? Dahlia’s a merciless competitor and she doesn’t play nice.” “Don’t worry brother, I won’t hit [pc.himHer] much. Sloan, munitions!” The plush essyra fans her tails. Her fuzzy limbs gather huge amounts of snow at their feet. She quickly molds snowballs and hands them to her spouse. Oh stars, Grimald wasn’t joking! Your date takes you by the hand and tugs you behind a lamppost before the first salvo hits! His sister and in-law literally bombard your hideout with snow bullets.White projectiles fly next to your arm, skim near your [pc.isNaga |coils |[pc.isTaur |tauric hind |sides ] ], and over your head in quick succession. You barely have the time to gather some snow for a counterattack. “[pc.name], give me an opening,” Grimald barks, handing you three snowballs. You can hear the cogs and pistons in his hands rev with power — he’s readying a killer shot! Here goes nothing. You wait for Dahlia’s next shot to make your move. You leap out of cover, running through the snow before sliding behind a big snow pile. Sloan’s stray shots miss you twice, albeit narrowly. She’s far less accurate than Dahlia but she makes up for it with a greater rate of fire. You fire your snowballs in quick succession, aiming Sloan, Dahlia, then Sloan again. If you dogpile one of them, you’ll be exposed to the other’s merciless retaliation. “Giving up already? I expected more from your brother!” Grimald peeks out of his cover for a second before setting his eyes on you. He doesn’t need words to carry his thoughts: “I need one more round to make it, so make it count!” You simply nod and prepare three more snowballs. It’ll be all in now. “Nice try, Dahlia, but I know you too well. You never show mercy, it’s against your nature. It’s always winning, never surrendering, never looking down, and I admire you for that. This is also why I can’t lose,” he says, rolling out of his hideout. His fingers flash with magmatic light before he unleashes a deluge of icy bullets! His snowballs fuse in impossible curves, crossing yards in a heartbeat! Dahlia and Sloan duck down, avoiding the worst of the punishment but one particularly low ball strikes the plump essyra square in the chest, knocking her down in the snow! “Sloan! Alright chicko, playtime’s over.” Dahlia leaps atop a snowpile and raises her arm for a thunderous shot! She dropped her guard, leaving her left side entirely exposed to a counterattack — now’s your chance. “Eat this!” Dahlia screeches. You suddenly rise and throw your snowballs at the furious anat and at her fluffy spouse. Grimald smirks a second before being hit by his sister’s projectile, knowing that things went just as planned. The blonde anat sees you in the corner of her eye but it’s too late. Two snowballs find her chest, scattering pristine snowflakes all over her bodysuit and face! Dahlia loses her balance, tumbling head over ass just like her brother a second ago. You keep the last one for Sloan. You dodge her snowy barrage and throw a curve ball, striking her big, soft belly! The spouses are profoundly stunned – how could they lose? What did they do wrong? “I-I can’t believe it. I didn’t see it coming.” The cheeky femboy giggles. “That was the plan, yes. I knew you’d see red if anything happened to Sloan, and I was right. A true warrior knows sacrifices must be made to win a child’s game.” You leave the siblings to their wares and head toward the big snow fox. “Are you alright?” you ask, offering her a hand. Sloan nods. “Yeah — a bit wet and staggered, but fine. Stars, I felt my entire body shake when Grimald hit me. He almost left me breathless.” “How dare you bully my wife — that’s my job!” Dahlia wryly pouts. “Dally please, don’t scream. We wanted to play hard and they played harder, that’s all.” The angry anat wants to retort but she knows her wife’s right. She took this a bit too seriously and got a run for her money. She was overconfident and paid for it. Grimald dusts himself a little and digs his sister off the snow. “Sorry for pushing your buttons, sis. It was the only way for us to win. Truce?” “Truce, but the drinks are on you,” Dahlia replies. “Fair enough,” Grimald smiles. “Sloan, [pc.name], how about some hot cocoa?”

You blink a couple of times, blinded by the bright lights. A weird, herbal scent lingers on your tongue. Strange, you don’t remember drinking or eating anything, nor being carried, stripped of your gear, and placed into a metallic box. However, you remember the cold: the deep, gnawing cold spreading through your veins, consuming your mind with its icy claws. “[pc.HeShe]’s waking up. How are [pc.hisHer] vitals?” an old, gruff voice asks. A familiar voice answers. “Oxygen saturation nominal, heartbeat steady, no signs of frostbite. We arrived just in time.” “Good job Grimald. I’ll let you take care of the rest.” Something clicks on the side. A moment later, the side panel flicks open, releasing you from the pressurized box. Grimald waits for you outside with a warm blanket and fuming cups of what you can only assume is hot cocoa. “[pc.name]? Is everything alright? Any numbness in your extremities or weight on your chest?” “No. W-What happened? I don-” Grimald helps you sit down and wraps the blanket around your shoulders. “Easy, no sudden moves. You were in severe hypothermia when we found you, but fortunately, you don’t seem to have any permanent damage. Your tests came out good but maybe we missed something, so take it easy. Want some cocoa?” “Y-Yeah. I’m still a bit cold.” The bird nurse gently hands you a hot mug. “Reflexes and coordination satisfying, no signs of shivering — you are recovering well,” he murmurs, reaching for his own mug. “Cheers.” Creamy cocoa warms your belly and rekindles your soul. Stars, it’s so hot and sweet, exactly what you needed to recharge your batteries! Each sip pushes the cold further away. Grimald slowly rubs your back. His augmented hands softly ripple, just enough to relax and heat you up in the kindest of manners. You sip the piping hot chocolate, unbothered by the medical room’s dry coldness, protected by the most conscientious of nurses. “Sorry for worrying you.” “It’s fine. I’m just glad you are okay. We brought you back to the temple and hooked you on life support in a heartbeat. You were so weak that the tranquilizers knocked you out, so we had to be extra careful, but in the end everything couldn’t have gone better: the pills worked as intended and the survival tank did wonders on your frozen cells.” You lean against him. “I had something to cling to, something that kept me afloat.” Grimald hides his blush behind his mug. “I-I see. Do you feel strong enough to stand?” “Only one way to find out,” you reply, taking a big sip. You put the mug aside and slide your [pc.isNaga |coils |legs ] of the bunk. You wag them a little, making sure everything’s fine down there before trying anything. Grimald wraps his arms around your chest to give you some support. “Ready?” “Ready,” you answer. The cold floor makes you shiver, but in a good way. You can feel the ground below you and, more importantly, your [pc.isNaga |naga tail holds |[pc.legs] stay firm and straight ], keeping you balanced and steady with close to no effort. You face Grimald and smile. “Looks like I’m good.” “Indeed. Let’s get you dressed.” “Can I finish the cocoa first?” you impishly ask. The feathered nurse nods. “Doctor’s orders: lots of hot stuff and thirty minutes of daily exercise.” That’s a program you can get behind. You quickly finish the hot chocolate before reaching for your shed belongings. Grimald helps you re-dress. His strong, chromed hands check your reflexes and agility while you get yourself more presentable. He’s taking his job very seriously — so seriously that he notes everything he observes on a checkup chart. When you are done, he turns the sheet around and hands you the chart. “Alright, just one formality left. I need your signature for the discharge, then you’ll be free to go.” You shrug. If Grimald took care of you, nothing should go wrong, right? You quickly read the surgical acts performed on you, checking the tests they undertook while you were out before signing the document. “Done. Can you escort me outside, please?” The half-anat smiles. “Gladly.” Grimald wraps his arm around yours and guides you out of the medical yard, then out of the temple. You cross a handful of hooded figures and armored stormguards that barely pay any attention to you, as if you didn’t exist at all. Weird... you’d expect more warmth from an old terran cult. At least they don’t annoy you. About three minutes in the tentacular building, you finally emerge outside. Gritting your teeth, you follow the fluffy boy to the exit, doing your best to stay stoic and brave against the freezing gusts. They aren’t as strong and cold as the ones that brought you down but they are still bone-chilling. “Be careful out there [pc.name]. I don’t want to lose you.” [flags.GRIMALD_SEX |You give him a kiss |You place a hand on his shoulder ]. “Promised.”

“Is it possible to hire Stormlancer bodyguards?” Grimald shakes his head. “Unfortunately no — not before a long time that is.” Why’s that a problem? “There’s been an incident with the locals a few months ago. A misunderstanding that led to repeated attacks against miners and fishers. Tribal raiders don’t care whether you are a tourist or a defiler, they’ll attack people on sight. Starfarers, lurelings’ marions, other korgonnes, descendants of the first settlers; it’s all the same to them. Protecting their land is all that matters to them, and I can understand that. If Pyrite landed in my backyard and presented me with one of their deals, I’d be on the warpath too.” All of their warriors are deployed in the Glacier then? Grimald nods. “Virtually everyone but the outpost guards and mobile patrols. You’d need a small fortune to book an off-schedule escort. We do provide individual rescuing services however. Our arctic nurses and medical shuttles are ready to lift off at a moment’s notice,” he says, glancing at his own ride. “Stormguards save people lost across the tundra?” “Yes,” the half-anat replies. “We charge a thousand credits per intervention to carry you back to Irestead and heal your frozen bones. We provide beacons to workers and adventurers who plan to cross the glaciers and frozen plains. They come with a life-support monitoring system that triggers it if you pass out from the cold or from exhaustion.”
Nevermind

original: A distant beeping rings in your frozen ears. It seems so far and so weak but it prevents you from completely dozing off. Stars, everything feels so cold, as if all heat left your body! Your eyes feel so heavy, so tired. Suddenly, loud turbines and flashing lights feel the arctic void. Hot winds batter your face as a white vessel with lightning patterns lands a few feet away from you. The rear panel opens a moment later, followed by a long, chromed ramp. “The signal’s coming from here. [pc.HeShe] must be close,” a rough, deep voice blares. Smaller flashlights scour the snow near you. “Here! I see something!” a more feminine ranger shouts. “Grimald! Keep the engines hot and ready the hardlight barrow!” Big, fuzzy hands dig the snow around you. They gently pass on your [pc.skinFurScales], wiping the icy flakes from your face before taking your pulse. “Don’t worry [pc.mf |buddy |missy ], we’ve got you. Erda! I need a thermal pack — quickly! Grimald, Orvil, help me carry [pc.himHer] to the ship!” The tender hands strap something to your chest. Suddenly, a miraculous warmth spreads through your frozen bones. You start shivering again — you still feel numb but you can feel your muscles and extremities again. It’s not enough to repel the cold but it’s enough to push back the dark torpor snaking around you. You blink. Your frosted eyelids feel so heavy. Everything looks so blurry but you recognize Grimald’s orange feathers and augmented hands looming over you. He examines you slowly, checking on your fingertips, [pc.isNaga |coils |[pc.feet] ], and chest before telling something to the big, burly dude. The stormguards lift you up a second later. They place you on a glowing stretcher, wrapping you tightly in a metallic blanket before carrying you inside the emergency shuttle. “Does [pc.heShe] need oxygen?” the deer woman asks. “No, [pc.heShe]’s breathing fine,” Grimald replies. “No frostbite or necrosis either, but we can’t be sure until we give [pc.himHer] a proper scan.” [flags.RESCUE_BEACON |“Grimmie, are you su-” “I’ve got this Uther. Take care of [pc.himHer] while I drive us back home. I’m counting on you guys.” |“Grimmie, do you know [pc.himHer]?” The half-anat nods. “Maybe it’s best if I pil-” “NO! No... I’ve got it, don’t worry Uther. Just watch over [pc.name] while I drive us back home. I’m not gonna lose her because someone else flew us too slowly across the tundra.” ] The fuzzy milodan shakes his head. “Alright, let’s go! Erda, Orvil, [silly |10cc of firewater!” “For [pc.himHer]?” his subordinate asks. “No, for me! Then prepare an ironheart infusion for this poor [pc.mf |guy |girl ]! |prepare some ironheart infusion for our patient! ]” he groans. “It’s two green pills and one yellow before ironheart infusion,” Grimald shouts from the driver’s seat. “Check [pc.hisHer] pulse before administering the yellow pill. If it’s over 80 bpm, skip the pill and give [pc.himHer] the infusion.” “Don’t worry, you’re in good hands,” Erda says.
